reviews.review_description: I was completely disappointed with this. these are just straight up pictures there's no interaction whatsoever you can't move you can't look around you're just staring at a pic. this is something you can quite literally do in vr internet. A total let down.

developer_response.body:
Hello, I am really sorry to hear that you were disappointed in this. The approach is about ‘slow technology’, trying to use the immersive space for something more calming, beautiful and meditative than conventional gaming. It is my solo project, and I shot it all around Australia and developed the app myself. We are not a large gaming enterprise, this is an indie production, but many people have found it to be a transcendent experience. 

I do think there is a digital content CGI overload that a whole new generation is accustomed to being overwhelmed by, and consequently, this experience may have left you deeply underwhelmed, unable to fully appreciate the detail and quality of the high resolution and colour managed photographic imagery on the VR platform, let alone the spiritual aspects communicated subliminally by the natural landscapes captured in immersive 360º VR panorama form.

However, as we offer users access to an unpaid trial, one has to ask oneself, why did you go ahead and purchase something that left you so disappointed?
That has now left me, the creator, completely disappointed in your executive processing as a potentially discerning consumer, as well as disappointed in the nature of creative endeavour, reception and criticism more broadly.
It’s all rather disheartening and disappointing, which is really even worse than your review.

I'd also really like to inform you that in 2025 Immersive Vision donated 10% of all sales to The Indigenous Literacy Foundation, to help disadvantaged Indigenous youth become literate as well as digitally equipped.

I'd also like to ask that you consider how this app might be very enjoyable to the elderly, people recovering in hospital as well as people with mobility challenges, who may never bee able to experience such places.

I ask, as you've left a consequential review, that you also take some time to look at the app once more, and consider the design elements, the visual still photography quality, the inclusion of both Indigenous and non-Indigenous place names, ad the general high GUI quality of the piece.

It didn't happen by accident.

I shot, stitched, optimised the photos, recorded landscape sounds on location, and engaged with and researched the Indigenous cultural importance of each location, all across the vast land of Australia. 

Then I conceived of a Virtual Gallery experience, and worked hard over six months to learn new programs (Unreal Engine) to have it work smoothly and beautifully for the user, considering the challenges some elderly and disabled may face when interacting, looking to share a beautiful experience of place and time.

I'd like to ask that, one day after a really stressful time at work, college or school, you take some time to sit with the app and consider the moments and sounds you are experiencing, and the beautiful colours captured by my high resolution professional camera.

Finally, with all these elements in mind, I'd really like you to reassess the rating that you have left for my work. It is of consequence,  and I'd really like you to ask yourself if that's truly the response you'd like to leave, give the information I have provided above. You are, and always were, welcome to request a refund, and it will be granted, but if you were to still leave the review there that feels rather unkind to me.

Immersive Australia is my first venture into the VR platform, and it may not be everybody's idea of how to utilise the space. But it is my dream realised, and it has deeply moved and helped many people, especially the elderly, the young and also the disabled.

I hope you continue to enjoy your Virtual experiences, and further expand your adventures with interesting and unusual developers in this space.

reviews.review_description:
Wasn’t exactly sure what to expect but I’ll be keeping this to visit these beautiful landscapes and soundscapes again in the future. Myall Lakes maybe my favourite. Beautiful 
country and photography.
What a beautiful way to experience photography. Elegantly designed. Soothing and transporting.

I could relax in this for hours. I backpacked Australia in 1992 and this brings back so many memories. I particularly like the beaches because the effects are so calming. Would like to see the same for US national parks and Scottish highlands.

This is more of an experience, with wellbeing in mind, you choose one of, at the moment 40 panoramic 360 areas, where you can chill and listen to the native sounds such as jungle and sea.  This can be sitting or standing but if sitting I recommend a chair that spins to take advantage of the 360 immersion. Perfect for relaxation, and experiencing the natural beauty of an environment.  This has bags of potential for other areas to be added.

Reminds me of a modern version of one of them Picture Viewers from the 80s.

Immersive 360, high quality photos, with sound effects from the locations.

A nice relaxing experience, and may bring memories back for those that have travelled to Australia.

reviews.review_title: I COME FROM A LAND DOWN UNDER
reviews.review_description:
Well I wasn’t sure what expect, it was a pleasant surprise. If you like an immersive photographic detail tour of Australia then you have it here.
Some way to move around would be nice, but I get what the dev is trying to do.
Create an art piece of Australia to enjoy.
Great for those who can’t travel and want to sit and enjoy a great immersive educational experience.

I was surprised at how many locations this included. Its like stepping in to Google maps, the sites are photographs so that was a little disappointing, would be better if there was some movement. But the sounds added in make it more realistic.

These beautiful immersive experiences are stunning, with the photography and soundscapes transporting to a very distant, remote land. What a pleasure to experience the different landscapes and soundscapes of Australia.
